description:
Grid Hamiltonian Path.

You are given two integers R and C, the number of rows and columns of a grid (cells are (row,col), 0-indexed). Return ANY one Hamiltonian path: an ordering of all R*C cells such that consecutive cells in the ordering are 4-directionally adjacent (differ by exactly 1 in exactly one coordinate) and every cell appears exactly once. If multiple valid paths exist, return any one of them; different correct submissions may return different paths.

stdin: a single line with two integers R and C.
output: R*C lines, each 'row col' giving the path in order.

Solution signature (Java): class Solution { public List<int[]> findPath(int r, int c) } where each int[] is {row, col}.
